mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.4pp unemployment rise, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and credit-dependent spending, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.5% force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening and erode real consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.5% are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive returns, with negative returns occurring in >50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue bases, which translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of instances, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening business investment, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
